# rustbase

> Opinionated Rust project template with Claude Code
> integration, quality gates, and CI/CD.

## Project Structure

Cargo workspace with three crates:

- `crates/rustbase` — Core library and CLI binary
- `crates/rustbase-web` — Axum web server (optional)
- `xtask` — Build automation (not published)

Frontend: Svelte 5 + Vite in `frontend/` (optional).

## Build Commands

```
cargo xtask validate          # fmt + clippy + tests + coverage + dupes + frontend
cargo xtask check             # fast compile-only check (no tests)
cargo xtask test [filter]     # tests only
cargo xtask clippy            # lint only
cargo xtask fmt               # format code
cargo xtask coverage          # coverage check (>=90% workspace, >=85% per module)
cargo xtask dupes             # code duplication check (<=6%)
cargo xtask deploy            # build + sync + restart on remote Linux+systemd host
cargo xtask deploy-setup      # one-time remote provisioning (user, dirs, service)
cargo xtask clean-cache       # drop stale target/{debug,release}/incremental/ content
```

`cargo xtask validate` is the canonical quality gate;
all PRs and the Stop hook run it. Coverage failures
include uncovered-line ranges per failing module, so
diagnosis doesn't need a separate detail command.

## Claude Code Skills

Slash commands shipped under `.claude/commands/`:

| Skill | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `/check` | Fast compile-only check (`cargo xtask check`) |
| `/test` | Run tests with agent-friendly output |
| `/validate` | Full quality pipeline with stepwise progress |
| `/commit` | Commit with versioning, diary, CHANGELOG, parallel Red Team + Artisan code reviews (with cross-confirmed-finding detection), and a post-commit workflow retrospective |
| `/retrospect` | Workflow retrospective on the session (Efficiency / Quality / Speed). Invoked automatically by `/commit`; also callable manually mid-session |
| `/todo` | Capture a work item with a generated slug into `docs/todo.md` (capture only) |
| `/implement` | Plan + execute a captured item. Phase 1 writes `docs/issues/<slug>.md`; Phase 2 codes with TDD; Phase 3 finalises and hands off to `/commit` |
| `/template-sync` | Pull improvements from the upstream rustbase template into derived projects; cross-references open divergences from `template-feedback.md` so documented differences aren't re-proposed |
| `/template-improve` | Log template feedback into one of three lifecycle sections (Open divergences / Resolved / Suggestions to flow back) |
| `/architect` | Project overview and architecture guide |
| `/web-dev` | Axum + Svelte 5 + Vite + Playwright patterns |

Code-review findings logged in
`docs/developer/redteam-{log,resolved}.md` and
`artisan-{log,resolved}.md` (open / resolved pair,
reverse-chronological).

## Template Sync

The repo tracks its template origin in
`.template-sync.toml`. Derived projects use
`/template-sync` to pull selected improvements
upstream-to-downstream while preserving local
customisations. The sync command consults the
project's own `template-feedback.md` "Open
divergences" section and auto-skips incoming changes
that conflict with a documented divergence.

## Web Server

The `rustbase-web` binary serves the Svelte frontend
and exposes a JSON API:

```
rustbase-web [OPTIONS]

Options:
  -p, --port <PORT>          Port [default: 3000]
  -b, --bind <ADDR>          Bind address [default: 127.0.0.1]
  -f, --frontend <PATH>      Frontend dist dir [default: frontend/dist]
```

### API Endpoints

| Method | Path           | Description            |
|--------|----------------|------------------------|
| GET    | /health        | Health check (JSON `{"status":"ok"}`) |
| GET    | /api/status    | Version and status     |
| GET    | /api/greeting  | Sample greeting        |

All non-API routes fall back to `index.html` for SPA
client-side routing.

## Quality Gates

- `#[deny(warnings)]`, `#[forbid(unsafe_code)]`
- Clippy pedantic + perf
- 90% line coverage minimum, 85% per module
- Code duplication <= 6% (production code, tests excluded)
- `cargo fmt` enforced
- Frontend: ESLint + Prettier + svelte-check, plus
  Vitest unit/component tests and Playwright E2E

## Build Profile

`[profile.release]` ships with `incremental = true`
and `codegen-units = 256`, tuned for fast iteration
on personal-use deployments. Override these for
performance-critical targets — see the comment block
in the workspace `Cargo.toml` for the trade-off
analysis and override recipe.

## Conventions

- Conventional Commits (`feat`, `fix`, `chore`, etc.)
  with AI-Generated footer
- Semantic Versioning (version in
  `crates/rustbase/Cargo.toml`)
- Keep a Changelog format in `CHANGELOG.md`
- Development diary in `docs/developer/DIARY.md`
- LF line endings, 80-char line width
- TDD: behaviour changes follow strict red/green;
  structural additions (new self-contained modules)
  may land test + impl together. See CLAUDE.md
  "Test-Driven Development" for the full rule.

## Removing the Web App

Delete `crates/rustbase-web/`, `frontend/`, and remove
`"crates/rustbase-web"` from `Cargo.toml` members.
